origin_reflect.description: CORS origin reflection — server reflects any Origin header

probes.description: Send request with Origin: https://evil.com, check if reflected in Access-Control-Allow-Origin
Suffix match bypass: Origin: https://target.com.evil.com — regex checks if ends with target.com

null_origin.description: CORS null origin bypass via sandboxed iframe

probes.description: Send with Origin: null — accepted by some CORS configs
Null origin from data: URI context — browsers send null Origin for data: URI navigations
Null origin from sandboxed iframe — sandbox attribute causes null Origin

credentials_wildcard.description: CORS wildcard with credentials — forbidden but misconfigured

probes.description: Check if Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true with wildcard origin

subdomain_wildcard.description: CORS trusting all subdomains — exploitable via subdomain takeover

probes.description: Origin: https://evil.target.com — test if subdomains trusted

pre_domain.description: Pre-domain bypass — Origin starts with target domain string, bypasses suffix regex

probes.description: Origin: https://targetcom.evil.com — server regex checks if origin ends with 'target.com' but misses dot

post_domain.description: Post-domain bypass — subdomain prefix, exploitable if subdomain takeover possible

probes.description: Origin: https://evil.target.com — server trusts *.target.com wildcard, attacker controls subdomain

special_chars.description: Special character origin bypass — underscore, backtick, browser-parser discrepancies

probes.description: Origin with underscore: https://target_com.evil.com — Safari treats underscore differently (CVE-style parser quirk)
Backtick bypass: Origin https://target.com%60.evil.com — URL encoded backtick in origin parsing

preflight_cache_poisoning.description: Browsers cache CORS preflight responses for `Access-Control-Max-Age` seconds (up to 7200 in Chromium, 86400 in spec). If a CORS bug is patched but Max-Age is high, browsers continue serving the poisoned preflight allowing exploitation in the cache window. Server-side: per-origin or per-path cache collisions.

probes.description: Send OPTIONS preflight, observe Access-Control-Max-Age. Values >= 600 = exploitation window for browser-side cache poisoning if any vulnerable variant exists.

Cross-path preflight cache — request OPTIONS on /api/public, then attempt CORS request to /api/admin. Browsers cache by origin+method+headers, NOT by path. If preflight covers admin via wildcard / loose policy, attacker reuses cached approval.
Vary header missing on preflight. Server should `Vary: Origin` so caches discriminate. Without it, intermediary cache may serve attacker-origin response to victim.
Access-Control-Allow-Headers reflection — preflight reflects requested headers without filtering. Combined with credentialed-wildcard, expands cross-origin attack surface.
